ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general coffee beverage class; no specific cold/iced coffee subclass exists. closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers coffee as consumed generically; no cold-specific subcode exists. closest_parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34718004 refers to coffee as a substance; no iced/cold coffee concept exists. closest_parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cup of cold black coffee, approximately 240 ml, as typically served in a Singaporean coffeeshop or café.

nutrient_source.title: Coffee, brewed, prepared with tap water (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, NDB 14209)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values are based on USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for brewed black coffee (NDB 14209 / FDC ID 171890). Cold coffee served without milk or sugar has the same nutritional profile as brewed black coffee per 100 g of liquid. Values adapted for cold-served presentation; no milk or sugar assumed for base record.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of beverages — black coffee contains no glycemic carbohydrates; GI conventionally assigned as negligible (analogous to plain tea and water, GI ~0–1)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Black coffee contains no digestible carbohydrates and therefore has no measurable glycemic response. The University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Augustin et al., 2015, Am J Clin Nutr) confirm that beverages with zero carbohydrate content have a GI of effectively zero. Per Veritas convention, glycemic_index is set to 1 (lowest valid positive value) and gi_category is 'low'. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate_g is 0.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (2 mg per 100 g); caffeine may cause transient blood pressure elevation
hypertension.guidance: Plain cold black coffee is very low in sodium and does not contribute meaningfully to dietary sodium load; however, individuals with hypertension should be aware that caffeine can transiently raise blood pressure and should moderate total daily coffee intake to 1–2 cups.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero glycemic carbohydrates; no impact on postprandial blood glucose when consumed without sugar or milk
type2_diabetes.guidance: Plain cold black coffee contains no digestible carbohydrates and has a glycemic load of zero, making it safe for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without added sugar or high-fat dairy; epidemiological evidence also suggests moderate coffee consumption may be associated with reduced T2D risk.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat and cholesterol; contains chlorogenic acids with antioxidant properties
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Plain cold black coffee is essentially free of saturated fat and cholesterol; moderate consumption (3–4 cups per day) has been associated with neutral to mildly beneficial cardiovascular outcomes in prospective cohort studies, though excessive intake may increase cardiovascular risk via caffeine-mediated effects.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cold Coffee (冷咖啡; Kopi Sejuk) is a chilled or iced black coffee beverage commonly served in Singaporean coffeeshops (kopitiams), cafés, and food courts. It is prepared by brewing coffee — typically using a blend of Robusta beans roasted with sugar and butter in the traditional Nanyang style, or using commercial espresso or drip methods — and then chilling or serving it over ice. When served plain and black, it contains virtually no calories, fat, or carbohydrates, making it one of the lowest-calorie beverage options available. The flavour profile is bold, slightly bitter, and aromatic, with the cold temperature softening the bitterness compared to hot coffee. In the Singaporean context, cold coffee may be ordered as 'kopi peng' (iced coffee with condensed milk) or as plain black iced coffee; this record covers the plain black cold variant without added milk or sugar. Nutritionally, plain cold black coffee provides negligible energy (approximately 1 kcal per 100 ml), trace protein (0.12 g), and minimal fat (0.02 g), with no glycemic carbohydrates. It is a source of caffeine (approximately 40 mg per 100 ml), potassium (49 mg per 100 ml), and B-vitamins including riboflavin and niacin. The glycemic index is effectively zero and the glycemic load per serving is 0.0, making it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease when consumed without added sugar or cream. Coffee also contains chlorogenic acids and other polyphenols with antioxidant activity. Clinically, moderate consumption is generally considered safe and may be associated with reduced risk of Type 2 Diabetes and certain neurodegenerative conditions.
common_user_queries: How many calories in iced black coffee?
Is cold coffee good for weight loss?
What is the GI of cold coffee?
Does cold coffee raise blood sugar?
Is cold coffee suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy brewed black coffee entry (FDC ID 171890), which is a well-validated approved source. The cold presentation does not alter the nutritional profile of plain black coffee. Confidence is medium rather than high because no Singapore-specific HPB Foodo entry for plain cold black coffee was identified as an exact match; the USDA brewed black coffee entry is the closest approved analogue.
notes: This record covers plain cold black coffee without added milk, sugar, or condensed milk. The food_name 'Cold Coffee' is the canonical English form; the input name 'Coffee 咖啡 (Cold/冷)' is preserved as an alias. Cuisine is classified as Singaporean because the source restaurant (Hai Kah Lang, Funan Mall) is a Singaporean establishment and cold coffee is a staple Singaporean beverage. Multilingual names follow Singaporean dish rules (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi). carbohydrate_g is 0.0: naturally zero, as brewed black coffee contains no digestible carbohydrates per USDA FoodData Central (confirmed zero). sugar_g is 0.0: naturally zero for plain black coffee with no added sugar (confirmed zero per USDA). dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: naturally zero for a liquid coffee beverage (confirmed zero per USDA). saturated_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for brewed black coffee. trans_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per Veritas convention for zero-carbohydrate beverages. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all reported from USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 171890. No -1 sentinel values are used in this record as USDA provides values for all reported fields; all fields with 0.0 are confirmed zero per the cited source.
